# Algorithmic Evidence Boundaries

## What public math can prove
- If a ranking system is monotone in a manipulable signal, that signal can alter rank.
- If exposure and engagement form feedback loops, early boosts can multiply.
- If users respond to visible social proof, fake proof can recruit real behaviour.
- If repeated co-action exceeds a null model, the pattern is unlikely under independence.

## What public math cannot prove alone
- Intent of a specific actor.
- Exact private platform weights.
- Whether a particular campaign caused all observed growth.
- Whether coordination was deceptive rather than openly organized.

## Best wording
Use: “consistent with coordinated amplification,” “more likely than under the null model,” “mechanism is plausible and empirically supported.”

Avoid: “mathematically proves fraud” unless there is direct admission, regulator finding, or internal record.
